entry.title: issue while updating pricing conditions for NBIC Purchase Orders using API_PURCHASEORDER_2
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue while updating pricing conditions for NBIC Purchase Orders using API_PURCHASEORDER_2 in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Scenario:

* PO Type: NBIC
* API: API_PURCHASEORDER_2
* Entity: PurOrderItemPricingElement
* Condition Type: PMP0

I am able to successfully PATCH the pricing condition using Postman and receive HTTP 200 OK response, but the value is not actually updated in the database. After re-reading the pricing element, the old value is restored automatically.

PATCH URL:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaseorder_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/PurOrderItemPricingElement(
PurchaseOrder='4500000450',
PurchaseOrderItem='00010',
PricingDocument='1000000667',
PricingDocumentItem='000010',
PricingProcedureStep='060',
PricingProcedureCounter='001'
)

Payload:

{
"ConditionRateAmount": 159.000000000,
"ConditionCurrency": "AED",
"ConditionIsManuallyChanged": true
}

Response:

* HTTP 200 OK
* No API error

However, after GET:

* ConditionRateAmount still shows old value
* Pricing seems to be redetermined automatically

Observed fields from GET response:

{
"ConditionControl": "C",
"ConditionOrigin": "C",
"ConditionIsDeletable": false,
"ConditionType": "PMP0"
}

Additional findings:

1. POST on PurOrderItemPricingElement is not supported
Error:
"Creating operations are disabled for entity"

2. PATCH on PurchaseOrderItem with NetPriceAmount also returns:
"Operation is not enabled"

3. Same API update works correctly for NB Purchase Orders, but not for NBIC.

My questions:

1. Is pricing condition update intentionally restricted for NBIC Purchase Orders in Public Cloud?
2. Are PMP0/intercompany conditions protected from API persistence?
3. Is there any supported approach to update pricing for NBIC POs through API?
4. Should pricing only be updated indirectly through condition records/info records?

Any guidance or SAP documentation reference would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: Transformation Journey with IFRS 18
entry.content:
IFRS 18: The new Accounting Gold Standard

The International Accounting Standards Board (IASB) issued IFRS 18 Presentation and Disclosure in Financial Statements in April 2024 to replace IAS 1. IFRS 18 improves the clarity and comparability of financial statements by introducing a mandatory structure for the statement of profit or loss. It requires entities to classify income and expenses into five defined categories - operating, investing, financing, income taxes, and discontinued operations while also introducing new disclosure requirements for management defined performance measures. The standard is effective for annual reporting periods beginning on or after 1 January 2027, with early adoption permitted and retrospective application required.

How does IFRS 18 impact Customers?

For customers, IFRS 18 will impact multiple areas in their current system set-up and would typically require adjustments to, but not limited to - chart of accounts design, G/L account assignments, financial statement version structure, updated cash flow statement, changes in group reporting configurations and to generate comparative financial statement version for previous year.

While S/4HANA’s Universal Journal provides a solid foundation to accommodate these changes, customers will need to proactively review configurations, calculations, and governance to ensure compliant and consistent reporting across entities. This will require early and proactive engagement with auditors to support the timely adaptation of the organization’s accounting policies in accordance with IFRS 18.

IFRS 18 should be approached as a business transformation initiative, not an IT project. For customers, it drives significant changes to financial reporting processes, data models, and governance frameworks, with IT enabling these changes rather than leading them.

Transition to IFRS 18

Customers should treat the adoption of IFRS 18 in an SAP Cloud ERP, SAP Cloud ERP, Private environments as transformation project. A typical IFRS 18 Transformation project could look as below:

We will summarize the steps for each phase but not limited of this transformation journey to in the below section.

Discover Phase

This is one of the most important phases for IFRS 18 Transformation. Some of the key activities that could be executed in Discover Phase.

Early Alignment with Auditors:

The adoption of IFRS 18 will significantly impact the organization’s financial reporting, including the requirement to present FY 2026 and FY 2027 comparatives under IFRS 18 despite prior audits. Early engagement with auditors is therefore critical to align on interpretation and application.

Key focus areas should include updates to the Chart of Accounts, general ledger structure and posting logic, alignment on the treatment of foreign exchange gains and losses, and an auditor‑aligned approach to restating or reclassifying prior‑year information to ensure comparability.

Understand Different Options Available for P&L Classification as per IFRS 18:

The introduction of IFRS 18, with its required classification of income and expenses into Operating, Investing, and Financing categories, will necessitate a thoughtful review of the Chart of Accounts (COA) and may require more granular GL accounts to enable accurate and consistent mapping. A clear understanding of how P&L accounts are posted and the underlying nature of the transactions should drive their appropriate categorization under IFRS 18, thereby reducing judgement, manual adjustments, and classification inconsistencies

For more information, refer to the Help Documentation

SAP Cloud ERP, Private: Activating Cost of Sales Accounting

SAP ERP: Activating Cost of Sales Accounting

In line with IFRS 18 requirements for consistent presentation of expenses by function, the functional area can be automatically derived in the accounting document through appropriate assignment within both process-related configurations and relevant master data.

For more information, refer to the Help Documentation:

SAP Cloud ERP, Private: Deriving Functional Area

SAP Cloud ERP: Deriving Functional Area

SAP ERP: Deriving Functional Area

Organizations already using Cost of Sales Accounting shall ensure that any revision to functional areas aligns with IFRS sub‑categorization requirements while continuing to support all existing statutory, management, and operational reporting needs without disruption.

Financial Statement Versions & Cash Flow Statement (Indirect Method):

IFRS 18 introduces new mandatory subtotals, such as Operating Profit, requiring existing Financial Statement Versions (FSVs) to be updated or replaced to align with the new five-category structure.

These changes affect global hierarchies used in Fiori reporting. Using the Manage Global Hierarchies app, customers can maintain FSV hierarchies based on G/L accounts, functional areas, or a combination of both, to support IFRS 18–compliant reporting.

Depending on the SAP release version, certain customers are able to configure Financial Statement Versions (FSVs) in a way that supports IFRS 18–compliant reporting requirements

SAP would deliver a sample Financial Statement Version, which will be released as specified in the below notes:

SAP Cloud ERP, Private: 3696338 - Advisory Note on IFRS 18 Transition in S/4HANA Private Cloud, S/4HANA - SAP for Me

SAP Cloud ERP: 3694359 - How to Adopt IFRS 18 for Financial Reporting in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

SAP would also provide a new Fiori Application for Cash Flow statement (Indirect Method) for SAP ERP Cloud, Private. Please refer to the Roadmap Explorer for more details.

Scope items 1GA will also be updated to include reference content for IFRS 18, which can be adapted as per customer requirements across SAP Cloud ERP and SAP Cloud ERP, Private.

Treatment of Foreign Exchange Gain or Loss:

According to IFRS 18, foreign currency exchange gains and losses must be presented in separate categories. The category of FX Gain/Loss is decided by the nature of the items from which gain or loss arise. For example:

  • FX Gain/loss arising from Trade Receivables/Payables --> Operating Category
  • FX Gain/loss arising from Cash and Cash equivalents --> Investing Category
  • FX Gain/loss arising from Issued bonds, borrowings --> Financing Category

As part of the IFRS 18 transformation, the nature of postings to each GL account must be analysed to determine whether a GL requires bifurcation to avoid mixed transaction types and ensure compliant presentation. Where new GL accounts are introduced, their foreign exchange gain or loss treatment must be reassessed to ensure accurate currency remeasurement. Accordingly, foreign currency valuation configuration must be adjusted based on whether the classic or advanced foreign currency valuation approach is being used.

When advanced foreign currency valuation is activated, valuation rules can include multiple steps, each using semantic tags to classify monetary items and assign the corresponding unrealized gain or loss accounts.

If you continue to use Classic Foreign Currency Valuation and do not plan to migrate to Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation prior to IFRS 18 adoption, you may update the unrealized gain/loss G/L account for transaction key KDF using transaction code.

Postings generated through Treasury & Risk Management (TRM):

Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) generate diverse profit and loss items that must be accurately classified as operating, investing, or financing to comply with IFRS 18.  For further details on TRM, refer to the SAP community blog post:  

IFRS 18 Compliance for Treasury and Risk Management in SAP Cloud ERP: Key Impacts and Approaches

Group Reporting:

For more details on how Group Reporting can support your IFRS 18 adoption journey, please refer to SAP Community blog post: 

IFRS 18 and SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting: What It M... - SAP Community.

Comparative Financial Statement Version:

Because IFRS 18 requires comparative statement, the 2026 data (comparatives for 2027) must be reportable in the new format. For more details, please refer to blog:

IFRS 18 Compliance: Generating Comparative P&L Rep... - SAP Community

Prepare Phase

In the context of an IFRS 18 transformation project, once the impacted reporting and finance areas are identified during the Discover phase, the Prepare phase involves setting up the project plan, identifying the relevant business and IT teams, and provisioning a sandbox environment to execute and validate the required changes.

Create a Plan:

Create a concise, IFRS 18–aligned critical‑path plan covering Chart of Accounts (COA) redesign, Cost of Sales activation (where applicable), Financial Statement Version (FSV) updates, and Foreign Exchange reconfiguration to enable the new presentation, aggregation, and disclosure requirements.

Identify Teams:

An IFRS 18 transformation requires coordinated involvement of Project Management, Accounting Policy Owners, Controllers, Finance Operations and FP&A, Management Performance Measure owners, IT and Reporting systems teams, Audit and Controls (internal and external) and Change and Training teams to ensure compliant financial statement presentation, robust disclosures, system readiness, and clear stakeholder communication.

Provision for a sandbox environment:

Create and maintain a sandbox environment using a recent copy of production data to enable early prototyping.

Explore Phase

This is the most critical phase of this project. Key activities that should be executed in Explore Phase.

Mapping COA to IFRS Categories:

Map every existing P&L account to the mandated IFRS 18 categories: Operating, Investing, Financing, Income tax and discontinued operations. Assignment of a P&L account to a particular category will depend on the nature of transaction from which this posting is generated.

As part of the IFRS 18 transformation, when Cost of Sales accounting is activated, the required functional areas must be clearly defined together with robust and transparent derivation rules to ensure consistent classification of expenses by function.

List down any GAP identified during the mapping exercise.

Create/Update of Financial Statement Version (FSVs) and Cash Flow Statement (Indirect Method):

As part of the IFRS 18 transformation, identify and agree the new Financial Statement Version (FSV) format with the auditor, ensuring clear segregation of operating, investing, financing, tax, and discontinued operations categories. The FSV structure should leverage functional areas, where available, and align with the new sample FSVs and the Cash Flow Statement (Indirect Method) app introduced during the Discover phase to ensure compliance and consistency across financial statements.

FX Gain/Loss Changed due to bifurcation of underlying GL accounts:

If the Chart of Accounts (COA) are going to have new set of GLs based on nature of postings, Foreign currency valuation configuration will also be changed. List down all the GLs for which Foreign currency valuation configuration needs to be updated. Define the posting rules based on advance or classis FC valuation approach you are using.

Comparative Financial Statement Version:

IFRS requires comparative statements to be presented with 2026 data restatement. Please refer to the blog:

IFRS 18 Compliance: Generating Comparative P&L Rep... - SAP Community

Thereafter, decide the approach to be taken. Once finalized, you may need to add additional extension ledger or other solution based on your ERP version and the approach chosen.

Document the Design:

After finishing the mapping files, make a simple design document. In this document, list each area clearly, such as GL requirements, the new FSV structure, cash flow changes, and FX impacts.

At this stage, you should also create a list of gaps where the standard solution is not enough to meet your needs.

Realize Phase (Configuration & Testing)

During the Realization phase, we will implement the approved design by configuring the system, creating required master data, developing custom enhancements (if needed), and testing the solution. This work will support the new IFRS 18 presentation and disclosure requirements, especially around income statement structure, operating categories, and cash flow reporting.

We will create new GL accounts and update the Chart of Accounts, ensuring correct assignment to account determination settings. Cost of Sales Accounting will be configured, and the required functional areas will be defined and carefully derived to support IFRS 18‑aligned income statement presentation.

A new Financial Statement Version (FSV) will be created using transaction OB58 or the Manage Global Hierarchies Fiori app to reflect the updated IFRS 18 structure (operating, investing, and financing categories).

The Fiori Cash Flow Statement (Indirect Method) app will be activated, or a custom report will be developed if defined in the Discover Phase, to support enhanced cash flow disclosures under IFRS 18.

Where transaction-level detail is not available, new or updated allocation rules will be configured to reclassify amounts from one GL account to multiple reporting GLs, ensuring correct IFRS 18 categorisation.

If required, Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation will be configured using valuation rules and semantic tags, or Classic Foreign Currency Valuation (OB09) will be adjusted, in line with reporting requirements.

System Validation (SIT & UAT):

Below are some examples of activities during this phase but not limited to:

  • Validate that the transactions generated are now posted to correct setup of GLs as per nature of transaction.
  • FSV is generated as per required and agreed reporting format.
  • Derivation of functional areas for different processes is correct and complete.
  • Posting of FX Gain/Loss is posted correctly to correct set of GLs
  • Cash Flow Statement (Indirect Method) is working as expected.
  • Simulate month end and quarter end closing to validate that there are no unexpected issues coming.

In this phase active involvement of accounting and finance team is crucial to make the transition successful.

Deploy & Run Phase

Below are some activities but not limited to, that could be considered in this phase:

Cutover Activities:

Some of the cutover activities related to IFRS 18 Transformation Project could be, but not limited to:

  1. Move all required system changes (standard and custom) to the system in the correct order
  2. Create or update basic financial data, such as new GL Accounts, Assignment of Cost Centres to correct functional categories, New Cost Element Groups etc
  3. Switch on the Cash Flow Statement app and verify it works correctly
  4. Check that all automatic accounting rules (derivations and substitutions) are correctly set
  5. Make sure all cost allocations (allocations between departments or functions) are correctly updated
  6. Activate Cost of Sales Accounting so expenses are reported by business function
  7. Update user access and roles so users can see new reports and apps

Conclusion

IFRS 18 is the biggest change to how financial statements are presented since IFRS began. While it does not change how revenue or assets are measured, it significantly changes how income and expenses are grouped and shown in the financial statements.

IFRS 18 applies from 1 January 2027, and companies need to start preparing now to produce comparative information for 2026 and avoid implementation challenges

Credits to Co Author: @Pradeep_M

entry.title: Not Able to Change the status of ChaRM
entry.content:
We have been trying to change the status of below CR from To Be SIT tested to To Be UAT tested and further, but it is not progressing to the next setup and gets reset to Being implemented.

Even though the object is transported to QA environment, CR status is moved to being implemented.

ERROR : Transport request xxxx has not yet been imported into system aaa

This appears to be a generic issue for all the minor CRs created for change cycle bbb at least.

Message No./TMWFLOW/TRANSPORT_N631


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Not Able to Change the status of ChaRM
entry.content:
Regarding your issue, it seems due to your configurations for your custom transaction type YMHF is improper.

For user status E0004(To be UAT Tested), you have assigned action 'SAVE_PARTNER' only, but if you compare with standard urgent change SMHF, you will see for user status E0004, the assigned actions include 'RELEASE_REQU' and 'IMPORT_TEST' as well, these actions will release/import TRs into next target system, in consistency check, there is 'IMPORT_INTO_TEST_OK' check to see if the TR is imported into QA system or not.

So kindly verify/adjust your YMHF configurations according to standard SMHF.

entry.title: Re: Provide the technical ID for test step attachments in...
entry.content: It is not possible to upload new attachments. This functionality allows to upload Test Cases with existing attachments and keeping them stable in newer versions of your test case. That is, once you download a test case, the attachments IDs will be included in the file and can be used via upload later again. (in older SPs this was not possible and thus you lost the attachments when uploading a new test case version.) These are based on what is already available in Test Steps Designer / Solution Manager Knowledge Warehouse The ID is basically the LOIO-id (OBJID) of an already existing Step or Header Attachment (listed in table /SALM/TM_TD_SATT or /SALM/TM_TD_HATT)

entry.title: Grey Out “Message Processor” Field During Incident Creation in ITSM
entry.content:
Hi,

We are looking for a functionality in ITSM to grey out the “Message Processor” field when a user creates an incident.

During incident creation, the user should only be able to select the Support Team. The Message Processor field should not be editable at this stage.

Once the incident is created, the HOD will review the incident and assign the Message Processor as per the selected Support Team.

Kindly let us know if this functionality is available in standard ITSM and please help with the implementation approach.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Grey Out “Message Processor” Field During Incident Cr...
entry.content: In SAP standard, we have SPRO->SAP Solution Manager->Capabilities->IT Service Management->Define Partner Determination Procedure->select your incident transaction type, standard one is SMIN0001->in the left sidebar, select 'Partner functions in procedure'->there you could change the field 'message processor' to uneditable by uncheck the checkbox 'changeable'.

entry.title: Enhancing SAP SuccessFactors User Experience with WalkMe Digital Adoption Platform
entry.content:
Introduction:

Walkme is a digital adoption platform that provide smart embedded walkthroughs across SAP Modules in the SAP SuccessFactors.No more boring user manuals , videos , with WalkMe smart embedded Walkthroughs , the user experience is greatly enhanced!

How a Quick 2 Minute WalkMe Setup does so much to the User Experience!

Step 1 :  Enter WalkMe Digital Adoption Platform in the Search header 

Step 2 : Enable the Toggle and Choose SAP SuccessFactors Managed Content

Two types of WalkMe Content:

  • Customer-Managed Content: This allows customers/companies to create WalkMe guidance tailored specifically to their employees, using their own policies, processes, and system configurations. This option requires a WalkMe license and at least one connected system. For more information, please refer to Help Portal - Showing Your WalkMe Content to Your Users
  • SAP SuccessFactors-Managed Content: SAP SuccessFactors provides built-in WalkMe content that's available without a WalkMe license. This content is created and maintained directly by SAP SuccessFactors.

Users will now see ? in the Page , which will show Walkthroughs based on the context of the Page.

 

My Personal Experience :

WalkMe has been enabled in one of my Customer Instance during the initial release and response was so good from the client. The Embedded Walkthrough has added personal touch to the user experience and it has only grown from the initial release with additional walkthroughs getting added timely.The standout feature is the context aware walkthrough which intelligent understands the page and provide walkthrough based on the module.

Conclusion : 

With just a quick setup, WalkMe transforms the SAP SuccessFactors experience by embedding intelligent, contextual guidance directly into the application. This modern approach to digital adoption improves usability, and increases overall user productivity.

entry.title: Extending Data Replication Proxies to Third-Party Payroll Systems
entry.content:
Background

Many organizations use Employee Central Payroll (ECP) in countries with large employee populations. In these scenarios, evaluated time results — calculated from timesheet information — are replicated using Data Replication Proxy objects, providing a reliable mechanism to transfer time data to payroll.

In countries with smaller employee populations or where SAP Payroll is not offered, companies often use non-SAP payroll solutions. However, the Data Replication Proxy functionality was previously only available when ECP was licensed, making it unavailable for third-party payroll integrations. This gap meant customers had to build custom integrations, which required significant effort and often led to unreliable data transfer. In some cases, this resulted in incorrect payroll calculations — a challenge we heard loud and clear from our customers.

------------

What this enables — and what's next

Opening the Data Replication Proxy framework is the foundational building block. To match the reliability customers know from ECP, the integration also needs a defined persistence and confirmation pattern on the integration side. We're working on end-to-end guidance — published as an Implementation Design Principle (IDP) — that covers the full pattern, not just the API surface.

------------

 

What's New

We're bringing the proven change-tracking foundation of Data Replication Proxies to third-party payroll systems, making hybrid payroll landscapes easier to manage.

 

How It Works

Activation: A new switch in Manage Employee Central Settings called Time Data Replication to Third-party Payroll Systems enables this functionality. This requires a SuccessFactors Time Tracking license (not an ECP license).

Data Access: Once enabled, Data Replication Proxies are created for time data changes. Third-party systems can query these proxies using an OData-based API.

Confirmation: A REST API allows external payroll systems to confirm when they have successfully processed a proxy, maintaining data integrity.

Key Difference from ECP: The Data Replication Monitor is not available for third-party integrations. Monitoring is handled through the integration platform rather than a built-in tool.

Opening the proxy framework solves the data-access half of the problem. Building a reliable payroll integration on top of it — handling state, confirmations, and edge cases such as proxy recreation — is where the upcoming IDP guidance comes in.

 

Why This Matters

  • Proven Foundation: The same change-tracking mechanism ECP customers rely on, now available as a building block for third-party scenarios.
  • More Standardized Integrations: Move away from fragile workarounds like custom Integration Center jobs or reporting-based solutions toward a standard, API-based approach.
  • Better Accuracy: Reduce the risk of payroll calculation errors due to missed or incomplete time data.
  • Flexible Architecture: Support diverse payroll landscapes with a consistent integration pattern.
    Availability


This feature is not yet generally available. Customers interested in using it can request activation.

We're actively developing end-to-end implementation guidance covering proxy consumption, persistence, confirmation, and error handling. The Implementation Design Principle document will be published once available.

entry.title: Re: The S-user account has been created, but it cannot su...
entry.content:
In order to create, close, or reply to SAP support cases an S-user ID needs case related authorizations. These can be assigned on different levels depending on the customer's needs (CCC level, customer number level, installation number level etc.).

The required authorizations are as follows:

  • Report Technical Problem
  • Close Cases (see KBA 2646108)
  • Send Cases to SAP (see KBA 2656179)
  • Display Cases
  • Display all Cases
  • Display Cloud Data (applies to SAP Cloud products only, e.g. SuccessFactors, refer to KBA 2598170)

These authorizations can be assigned by an administrator that has the case handling authorizations mentioned above. To request the desired authorizations use the self-service process described in the KBA 2891528.

entry.title: Principal Propagation from SAP Build Process Automation workflow to S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

When building automated workflows in SAP Build Process Automation, a common requirement is to call backend APIs in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud on behalf of the user who triggered the workflow, not a generic technical user. This is where principal propagation becomes essential.
Principal propagation ensures that the identity of the originating user is securely forwarded through the call chain, allowing S/4HANA to enforce its own authorization checks based on who actually initiated the action. Without it, all requests reach the backend under a shared service account, which undermines auditability, segregation of duties, and fine-grained access control.
In this article, I walk through the exact configuration steps I followed to enable principal propagation between SAP Build Process Automation and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, covering the trust setup on SAP BTP, the destination configuration, and the corresponding communication arrangement on the S/4HANA side. If you are facing the same challenge, this guide should give you a clear, step-by-step path to get it working.

Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure you have:
  • An SAP BTP subaccount with SAP Build Process Automation enabled
  • Access to your SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system with an administrator role
  • An existing workflow in SAP Build Process Automation that needs to call an S/4HANA API

Step 1 — Configure S/4HANA Public Cloud

1.1 Create a Communication User

In the SAP S/4HANA Fiori launchpad, navigate to Communication Management > Maintain Communication Users and create a dedicated user for inbound communication. Make sure you save the password; this will be needed in configuring the Destination.

1.2 Create a Communication Scenario

Navigate to Communication Management > Communication Scenarios and either select an existing scenario that exposes the API you need or create a custom one. Note the scenario ID — you will need it in the next step.
Regardless of whether you use an existing or custom scenario, make sure that OAuth 2.0 is enabled as a supported authorization method under the inbound communication settings of the scenario. This is a prerequisite for the SAML bearer assertion flow to work - if OAuth 2.0 is not listed as an authorization option in the scenario, the Communication Arrangement will not allow you to configure it, and principal propagation will not be possible for that API.

1.3 Create a Communication System

Before creating the Communication System in S/4HANA, you first need to export the trust certificate from BTP.
 
In the SAP BTP Cockpit, navigate to Connectivity > Destinations, scroll down to the Destination Trust section, and click Export to download the certificate file. The file will be in .pem format — keep it ready for the next step.
 
In your SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system, navigate to Communication Management > Communication Systems and create a new system. Set the Host field to your BTP subaccount subdomain.
 
Under the OAuth 2.0 Settings section, enable OAuth 2.0 Identity Provider and upload the .pem file you just exported from BTP. Once uploaded, the certificate details will be displayed — locate the CN (Common Name) field, copy its value, and paste it into the OAuth 2.0 SAML Issuer field.
 
This links S/4HANA's trust configuration to your BTP subaccount and is what allows S/4HANA to validate the SAML assertions that BTP will generate during principal propagation.
 

 

1.4 Create a Communication Arrangement

Navigate to Communication Management > Communication Arrangements, create a new arrangement, and link it to the communication scenario, communication system, and communication user created in the previous steps.
 
Note - User for inbound communication must be added with OAuth 2.0 Authentication Method. 

After save, you can see the OAuth 2.0 Details. These will be used in setting up the Destination in BTP.

Step 2 — Configure the BTP Destination

In your SAP BTP cockpit, navigate to your subaccount and open Connectivity > Destinations. Create a new destination with the following key settings:
 
Property Value
AuthenticationOAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion
AudienceYour S/4HANA system URL
Client KeyCommunication user name
Token Service URLOAuth token endpoint from the Communication Arrangement
Token Service URL Type DedicatedDedicated
Use Basic Credentials for Token Service✔️
Token Service UserCommunication user name
Token Service PasswordPassword for the Communication User created in Step 1.1
AuthnContextClassRef
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:PreviousSession
Name Id Format
urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress
User Id Source
email
Additional Properties
 
compactAssertion
true
 

 

 

Step 3 — Configure SAP Build Process Automation

Open your workflow in SAP Build Process Automation and locate the action step that calls the S/4HANA API.
In the step configuration, enable the option "Run on behalf of" and map it to the workflow initiator or the relevant user identity. This tells SBPA to pass the user context when invoking the destination, which, combined with the OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion destination and the SAML trust in S/4HANA, completes the propagation chain.

How It All Fits Together

Once configured end-to-end, the flow works as follows:
  1. A user triggers the workflow in SAP Build Process Automation
  2. The action step runs on behalf of that user and calls the BTP destination
  3. BTP generates a SAML assertion carrying the user identity and exchanges it for an OAuth token from S/4HANA
  4. S/4HANA validates the token against the Communication System trust and applies its own authorization checks for that specific user

Conclusion

Principal propagation is a few configuration steps across systems, but each piece is critical. Missing any one of them, such as not uploading the correct signature in the Communication System, will cause the setup to fail entirely.
 
If you run into issues, the most useful debugging step is to test the destination directly from the BTP cockpit using Check Connection and verify in the S/4HANA application log which user identity is being received.
 
I hope this guide saves you the trial and error. Feel free to drop questions in the comments.

entry.title: Re: How to Display Position Code in Latest People Profile Header – SAP SuccessFactors EC Workaround
entry.content:
Hi @rushdarizvie,

Good day!

As suggested by @fvt, one possible workaround is to create a new field (Ready Only) and configure an onView business rule so that only the required external code is displayed.

In addition, with the new 1H26 update, there is a new option called "Show External Codes" under Settings > People Profile Data Display. When this setting is enabled, external codes will be displayed on the latest People Profile. However, please note that this is currently an all-or-nothing setting, meaning all external codes will appear on the profile.

Thanks,
Arfat

entry.title: Introduction to Tendering(Sub-Contracting) in SAP Transportation Management
entry.content:
Introduction to Tendering in SAP TM 

Tendering refers to the subcontracting process in which a shipper or logistics service provider (LSP) offers a Freight Order (FO) or Freight Booking (FB) to one or more carriers and awaits their acceptance prior to awarding the business.
In SAP Transportation Management (TM), the carrier is defined as the business partner to whom the freight order is subcontracted. The executing carrier may differ; this distinction is relevant when the awarded carrier further subcontracts the transportation to a third party. This separation enables tracking of the contracted party versus the entity that physically moves the freight.
 
Tendering functions as the digital equivalent of a procurement cycle for freight, executed within the SAP TM document, and provides full traceability of all offers and responses.

Tendering in the end-to-end process flow

Tendering comes after planning/Optimization, when the FO or FB is built, and before execution(Refer Figure 1)

Figure 1

Now let’s focus on different process and steps in Tendering and see what actually happens inside it.

Tendering is a Six-Step Process, that takes a freight order,find the right carrier and updates the Freight order with Awarded Carrier

           

Step 1 Freight Order :  FO is created by the planner or automatically from freight units, with routes and stages defined, and the document is in the correct lifecycle status for tendering. No tendering actions occur at this stage; the FO serves as the input. Data integrity is critical, as all subsequent steps rely on the FO's information. Incorrect origin or destination details result in the wrong carrier pool at Step 2. Errors in weight, volume, or equipment requirements may exclude qualified carriers or include unqualified ones. Missing qualification flags, such as hazardous goods or temperature control, can lead to carriers accepting loads they cannot legally or operationally handle. The Freight Order is the source of truth. Any error at this stage will affect the entire tendering process.Incorrect data at Step 1 leads to an incorrect carrier pool at Step 2 and inaccurate rates at Step 3.

 
Step 2: Carrier Selection and Ranking. : Carriers are filtered based on eligibility criteria for the specified lane, mode, equipment, and operational constraints, then ranked by either transportation lane priority or carrier scorecard. The ranked list is used for the next tendering type, establishing the sequence for Peer-to-Peer tendering and defining the eligible pool for Broadcast
 
Step 3 : RFQ creation & publishing : At this stage, the freight order is sent to the carrier. SAP TM generates a formal Request for Quotation (RFQ) that includes pickup and delivery details, load specifications, special handling requirements, and the proposed rate. The RFQ is delivered as an email, an electronic EDI message, or a web portal notification (BN4L, CCP). The content remains the same; only the delivery channel varies. The recipient depends on the tendering type: one carrier at a time in Peer-to-Peer, all eligible carriers simultaneously in Broadcast, or any qualified carrier in the marketplace for Open/Spot.
 
Step 4 : Carrier quotation (FQ) : The carrier can accept, reject, or make a counteroffer. If the carrier accepts, the process is complete. If they reject, the tendering process starts again. If a counteroffer with a new price is allowed by the Tendering Profile, the process goes back to Step 5 for review.
 
Step 5 : Quotation evaluation & assignment : This explains how the award decision works. In Peer-to-Peer, the process is automatic and happens as soon as the first offer is accepted. For Broadcast, you can set it up so that either the first accepted bid wins or the best bid after the deadline is chosen. In Open or Spot, the process is usually manual, and a planner reviews all the bids in the Tendering Manager.
 
This 6-step overview also gives you a clear way to follow the rest of this blog series. Each type of tendering uses the same six steps, but Steps 3, 4, and 5 work quite differently:
 
StepPeer to PeerBroadcastOpen/Spot
3.RFQ Publishing 
Sent to carrier 1 only
Sent to all eligible at once
Published to B4NL
4.Carrier Quatation
Wait for carrier 1; if rejected, send to carrier 2
All carriers respond in parallel
Carriers self-select and bid
5.Evaluation&Assignment
First acceptance wins
First-accept or best-bid rule
Manual planner decision on bids
 
Steps 1, 2, and 6 are basically the same for all three types. The carrier selection logic and the FO update mechanics stay the same, while only the steps 3, 4, and 5 are different.
 
Understanding the difference in tendering between Basic and Advanced TM system
Before you start setting up tendering, make sure you know exactly what your TM license includes.
 
With Basic Shipping (Basic TM in S/4HANA), you can only confirm awards manually. There is no automated tendering engine, so the planner has to go through the subcontracting menu options step by step.
 
Advanced Transportation Management gives you a full automated tendering suite. This includes Peer-to-Peer, Broadcast, and Open (Spot) options, which can be sent directly by email or EDI, or through SAP SAP Business Network for Logistics (B4NL).
 
ScopeTendering CapabilityCommunication
Basic Shipping
Only manual award confirmation — planner sets statuses Manually: Send to Carrier → Set to Confirmed by Carrier
None automated
Advanced TM
Full automated tendering: Peer-to-Peer, Broadcast, Open / Spot
Direct (email / EDI) and via SAP B4NL
 
If you’re using Basic TM and your client wants automated tendering, you’ll need to discuss licensing rather than configuration.
 
Different Type of Tendering
SAP TM offers three types of tendering processes. Each type is designed to meet a specific business need.

Peer-to-Peer : system sends freight RFQs in sequence to the chosen carriers. There are two types: response required and no response required. If no acceptable quote is received, the process moves to the next step you have set up.
 
Broadcast tendering : system sends freight RFQs to all carriers at the same time. Depending on the settings, either the first acceptable quote wins, or after the response time ends, the system reviews all quotes and picks the lowest price.
 
Spot tendering : when a customer has yearly contracts with carriers but may also contact carriers without a contract.

A note on terminology: The standard term used in SAP TM is Open Tendering. The industry term Spot Tendering typically refers to the same concept — opening a load to the spot freight market, usually delivered through SAP B4NL Freight Collaboration. I'll use both terms throughout this series.

Status management during tendering 

Below is the Lifecycle,Subcontracting and Confimration Status in Freight order

Carrier communication channels

SAP TM can send a tender to a carrier using different channels. You can set up which channel to use for each carrier and business requiement
 

 

When SAP TM communicates a tender to a carrier, it can use any of the following channels. The choice is configured per carrier 

Behind the scenes, the integration service TM_ERPShipmentIntegration_In carries the Send Shipment and Notify of Tendering Result messages between SAP ERP and SAP TM — that's the technical glue that lets ERP know when a tendering result comes back.

 

Channel

Best for

Notes

SAP B4NL— Freight Collaboration

Modern S/4HANA TM deployments

Cloud-based, supports all three tendering types, carriers self-onboard

Email + PDF

Smaller carriers without IT integration

Low setup; carrier responds by clicking embedded reply link

EDI / IDoc

Mature LSP-carrier relationships

Bi-directional, fully automated

B2B XML / Web Service

Carriers with their own TMS

Real-time, programmatic

Carrier Collaboration Portal (CCP)

Older SAP TM 9.x landscapes

NetWeaver-based portal

Conclusion 

We have covered the basics of tendering in SAP Transportation Management: what it is, where it fits in the overall process, the six-step internal workflow, the difference between Basic and Advanced scope, the two main tendering types, the supported communication channels, and the TM business object model. With these key points in mind, you should now understand how SAP TM subcontracts a Freight Order to a carrier.
 

What's coming next in the series

Series

Topic

Focus

Series 2

Peer-to-Peer Tendering

Sequential carrier offers, priority logic, tendering profile config

**Version assumption: Configuration paths reflect S/4HANA TM (2021+). In SAP TM 9.x standalone, the customizing path sits under SCM Basis → Process Controller slightly different navigation, but conceptually the same objects.

entry.title: Transitioning from SAP Enable Now to the WalkMe Platform – A Brief Summary for SAP Enable Now Custom
entry.content:
For many SAP Enable Now customers, SAP Enable Now and SAP Companion have been critical components of their digital learning and in-application support strategies for many years. With the announcement of SAP Enable Now’s end of mainstream maintenance on November 30, 2030, it’s important that SAP Enable Now customers consider their approach to supporting business change and driving solution adoption, once Enable Now is no longer available.

This post introduces the solutions in the WalkMe platform, for existing SAP Enable Now customers, and provides important information about the transition timing and strategy.

What is the WalkMe platform?

WalkMe is a digital platform that helps organisations improve how people learn, adapt, and execute work across enterprise applications. The WalkMe platform consists of multiple solutions that allow customers to identify and resolve digital friction points in their system workflows and a single solution that supports digital learning.  WalkMe is a recognized global leader in this space.  Its solutions support outcomes such as faster onboarding, better process compliance, improved data quality, reduced support demand, and stronger return on software investments.

WalkMe systems are classified as either Digital Adoption or Digital Learning solutions.  Briefly, these solutions can be described as:

  1. WalkMe’s Digital Adoption solutions are designed to overlay enterprise applications, such as S/4HANA and SuccessFactors, to enhance the users’ experience of those applications.  Digital adoption solutions drive greater user productivity and capability by guiding users in application and automating common, repetitive tasks. 
  2. WalkMe’s AI-native Digital Learning solution was designed to improve the quality of training and ensure that critical content is always easily accessible by an organization’s employees.  The learning content can be distributed through a modern, searchable portal and is also available in application to allow easy, continual access by end users.

For SAP Enable Now customers, the WalkMe digital adoption solutions correspond to the solutions created and delivered using SAP Companion or SAP Companion for Desktop, e.g. Guided Tours and in-application message bubbles.  WalkMe’s digital learning solution corresponds to the solutions created in Enable Now Producer, including books, book pages, and simulations.

The WalkMe Digital Adoption solutions

WalkMe licenses its digital adoption solutions in two ways.  Digital adoption capability can be licensed for a single enterprise application, such as S/4HANA, or for an unlimited number of applications within an organization.  WalkMe solutions licensed for a single application are referred to as WalkMe Premium, e.g. WalkMe Premium for S/4HANA or WalkMe Premium for SuccessFactors.  When organizations license the WalkMe digital adoption capabilities for an unlimited number of applications, the solution is referred to as WalkMe DAP.

The WalkMe Premium and WalkMe DAP solutions offer comprehensive digital adoption capabilities for organizations that are undergoing significant change or plan to undertake major business transformations. These solutions allow organizations to understand actual user behavior, to build solutions to resolve friction points in complex user workflows, and to track and report changes in user efficiency and productivity. The WalkMe digital adoption capabilities are particularly valuable for businesses that are introducing new platforms, redesigning processes, or trying to increase value from complex application landscapes that span SAP and non-SAP systems.

Common business issues addressed by the WalkMe Premium and WalkMe DAP solutions include slow adoption of new technology, inconsistent process execution, underused software features, low employee productivity, and fragmented user experiences across systems.

WalkMe Premium and DAP solutions can be used to support SAP and non-SAP applications.  They can also be used to support web applications, such as S/4HANA, desktop applications, such as SAP GUI, and mobile applications.

WalkMe digital adoption solutions can be licensed to support a customer’s employees or a customer’s customers.  Examples of solutions used to support employee system usage are WalkMe Premium for S/4HANA and WalkMe Premium for Ariba.  The WalkMe solution used to support a customer’s customers is called WalkMe Premium for Customers.

The SAP Enable Now equivalent of the WalkMe digital adoption solutions is SAP Companion for web applications and SAP Companion for Desktop for desktop applications.  Think of the WalkMe solutions as SAP Companion on steroids.  There is definitely overlapping capabilities of the WalkMe solutions and SAP Companion but also capability available in WalkMe that cannot be replicated with SAP Companion, e.g. understanding actual user behavior to identify frictions in user workflows.

Figure 1 WalkMe Digital Adoption capabilities

 

 

 

 

 

 

The WalkMe Digital Learning solution

WalkMe Learning Arc is the WalkMe solution most directly aligned to the digital learning use cases historically addressed by SAP Enable Now, e.g. books, book pages, simulations and quizzes.  In WalkMe Learning Arc, content can be delivered as individual microlearning objects, including simulations, quizzes and screen recordings, or it can be structured into courses for delivery to end users.

WalkMe Learning Arc courses can be generated using AI.  The generated content can then be modified and additional content added by authors before being distributed to end users.  This approach to content creation is a significant enhancement on existing Enable Now capabilities.

WalkMe Learning Arc content can be delivered through a searchable company portal that contains the ability for users to quickly locate and access relevant content.  WalkMe Learning Arc content, such as simulations, can also be delivered in-application to ensure that it is available for end users as they work in the corresponding applications.

Figure 2 WalkMe Learning Arc AI course creation capability

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Retaining access to Enable Now Digital Learning content

When considering a transition to the WalkMe platform, it’s important that existing Enable Now Cloud customers determine if they wish to retain access to existing Enable Now digital learning content, such as books, book pages and simulations.  Customers that have invested time, effort and money into creating existing Enable Now digital learning content can continue to leverage that content by loading it onto the WalkMe Learning Arc platform.  Once loaded onto the WalkMe Learning Arc platform, the Enable Now content can be published and distributed to WalkMe Learning Arc end users, just like native WalkMe Learning Arc content objects.

Customers that load their existing Enable Now digital learning content onto the WalkMe Learning Arc platform can continue to update and supplement that content through an integration between WalkMe Learning Arc and Producer.  Enable Now content hosted on the WalkMe Learning Arc platform can continue to be updated, using the Producer integration, until November 30, 2030; even after a customer’s SAP Enable Now subscription has expired.  Following the November 30, 2030 end of life of Enable Now, the Producer integration will no longer be available but customers can continue to distribute, but not update, their Enable Now content through WalkMe Learning Arc.

If Enable Now customers wish to load their existing content onto the WalkMe Learning Arc platform, it’s critical that they license Enable Now and WalkMe Learning Arc simultaneously, i.e. both products must be licensed at the same time.  This is necessary to allow the Enable Now content to be loaded onto the WalkMe Learning Arc platform.  Once customers have loaded their Enable Now content onto the platform and tested that content, they can then allow their Enable Now licenses to expire, while still retaining access to the Producer integration.

Given that customers must subscribe to Enable Now and WalkMe Learning Arc simultaneously, to allow existing content to be loaded onto the WalkMe Learning Arc platform, a specific WalkMe Test and Go Live License, at a significantly reduced cost, is available to allow customers to perform the content transition and testing activities.  Please discuss this license with your SAP or WalkMe sales representative.

Figure 3 Retaining access to SAP Enable Now content after transitioning to WalkMe

 

 

 

 

 

When is the right time to transition to the WalkMe platform?

For many existing SAP Enable Now Cloud customers, November 30, 2030 seems a long way off, so no urgency to make a decision about the transition to WalkMe, right?  Unfortunately, April 30, 2027 is the actual date that most Enable Now customers should be considering when thinking about a WalkMe transition, not November 30, 2030.

While the SAP Enable Now Cloud solution will be available and supported until November 30, 2030, for customers with valid subscriptions, renewals of active subscriptions will not be possible beyond April 30, 2027.  This means that for SAP Enable Now Cloud customers, your Enable Now Cloud subscription, with an end date post April 30, 2027, will be Terminated on the end date of your contract.

Assuming no change to the April 30, 2027 final renewal date, one of three scenarios applies to existing Enable Now customers:

  1. Existing SAP Enable Now Cloud customers with a renewal date prior to April 30, 2027 have one final opportunity to renew their Enable Now Cloud subscription. 

    Customers that choose to renew their SAP Enable Now Cloud subscription should ensure that they agree on a contract end date that allows sufficient time to consider their future digital adoption and learning strategies and to transition to a new platform.  No further renewals of Enable Now Cloud are possible, beyond April 30, 2027.

    Customers with a renewal date prior to April 30, 2027 that have decided to transition to the WalkMe platform immediately, rather than renewing their Enable Now Cloud subscription, should consider the information provided above when determining their transition strategy and the WalkMe solutions that they plan to license.  Please allow sufficient time to transition, test, and deploy your existing Enable Now content on the WalkMe Learning Arc platform, before your Enable Now license expires.  Consider obtaining support from SAP, WalkMe, or a WalkMe partner if capacity constraints are likely to impact your ability to transition to WalkMe Learning Arc on your preferred timetable.

  2. Existing SAP Enable Now customers that do not have a renewal due prior to April 30, 2027, and who wish to extend their current contract end date, should contact their SAP CSM or sales representative to discuss the options for an early renewal.

    SAP Enable Now Cloud customers with contracts that end after April 30, 2027, and who do not plan to extend their current Enable Now Cloud contract, should ensure that they transition to their new platform prior to the end of their current Enable Now contract.

  3. SAP Enable Now Cloud customers with existing contracts who are interested in moving to the WalkMe platform prior to the end of their current Enable Now contract should discuss their transition options with their SAP CSM or sales representative.  These resources will be able to provide early transition options and additional insights to allow you to make the right decision for your organization.

Conclusion

The practical message is clear: SAP Enable Now customers should start thinking about their transition to WalkMe now.  Enable Now remains available and supported until November 30, 2030 for customers that are licensed; however, the final opportunity to renew your existing Enable Now Cloud licences is April 30, 2027.

Customers that wish to deploy in-application solutions that guide users and automate common, tedious tasks should consider relevant WalkMe Premium or DAP solutions.  These solutions allow customers to identify major frictions in their online workflows, and to develop and deploy solutions that resolve those frictions.

WalkMe Learning Arc allows customers to rapidly develop, in some instances using AI, and deploy learning content, including in application to ensure that employees can access the content continually as they work.  WalkMe Learning Arc provides the capability to continually reinforce the critical knowledge and skills that are essential to operating in a modern work environment.

Existing Enable Now customers must license WalkMe Learning Arc if they wish to retain access to their existing SAP Enable Now digital learning content, including simulations.  Customers with overlapping subscriptions of WalkMe Learning Arc and Enable Now Cloud can load their existing Enable Now content onto the WalkMe platform and distribute it to WalkMe Learning Arc end users.  They can also update their Enable Now content using an integration to Producer, launched from WalkMe Learning Arc.

Please contact your SAP CSM or sales representative with any further questions on this topic.  Good luck with your WalkMe transition and see you on the other side the fence!

entry.title: Looking for standard APIs for SAP Warehouse Management (WM) processes
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am currently working on an integration project involving SAP Warehouse Management (WM) and need to trigger core warehouse processes via APIs.

Specifically, I am looking for standard APIs to handle the following requirements:

  • Create Transfer Order (similar to transaction LT01): Creating a TO for a specific material and quantity.

  • Transfer Stock / Move Storage Unit (similar to transaction LT10): Moving stock from one storage bin/type to another.

Any documentation, function modules, or best practices you can share would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance,

Leelamohan
